figures.caption: Figure 5: Unconditional MNIST-Binary generations from our model.
Table 4: MNIST-Binary training configuration used for the main result.
Table 5: Fly Brain enhancer-generation configuration used for the main result.
Table 7: LM1B configuration used for the reported one-step text result.

Figure 2: Overview of the Coupling Model. Stage A learns a coupling from discrete sequences to Gaussian latents. Stage B trains a parallel decoder on the induced pairs, enabling generation from a single model evaluation.

Table 6: Raw entropy and generative perplexity values used in Figure 1 for one-step LM1B generation. For our method, we report the temperature sweep with fixed top-p = 0.95 and latent scale zscale = 0.8. Lower generative perplexity and higher entropy are preferred.

Figure 6: Entropy–perplexity tradeoff for one-step LM1B generation, reproduced from Figure 1. The appendix copy is included with the evaluation details so that the sampling settings and metric definitions can be read alongside the result.
Table 3: Summary of the experimental benchmarks used in the paper.

Figure 4: Guidance is efficient and effective in one-step generation. Left: Coupling Model achieves lower FID than the MDM baseline for CFG, classifier guidance, and reward fine-tuning while using far fewer function evaluations. Right: Coupling Model shows better quality–guidance tradeoff across scales.
